headline: Software Steadies as Chip Selloff and Mideast Risk Frame the Open
body_html:
Traders arrive to a market split squarely down the middle, with advancers and decliners running nearly even across our 200-name universe heading into the session. Overnight tone was cautious, as Asian markets traded choppily amid renewed doubts about artificial-intelligence spending and lingering Middle East tensions. The set-up is defensive: investors are weighing fresh US-Iran friction against firming expectations for tighter Federal Reserve policy.

Sector leadership tells the story. Software paced gains in the prior session, up more than 2 percent, with healthcare close behind near 2 percent. The damage was concentrated in semiconductors, which fell more than 7 percent, alongside softer readings in materials and industrials. Energy slipped even as crude found support from renewed strikes in the region.

Watch the open for whether the chip rout extends or stabilizes after several outsized single-name declines, including a drop of nearly 24 percent in one power-semiconductor maker. The crosscurrents are clear: oil firmer on geopolitics, gold easing on rate-hike bets, and a data-center narrative suddenly under scrutiny. Breadth, not headline indices, will be the early tell on conviction.

briefs.headline: Chip Names Lead Losses Into the Open
briefs.body: Semiconductors were the deepest laggard, sliding more than 7 percent as a sector. A power-chip maker tumbled nearly 24 percent, while a connectivity specialist and a memory producer both fell into double digits. The space remains the key downside watch at the bell.

briefs.headline: Software and Data Plays Buck the Trend
briefs.body: Even amid AI skepticism, software led the prior tape, gaining over 2 percent. A data-warehousing firm rose almost 10 percent and an observability vendor advanced more than 8 percent, with a database name and a security provider both higher. The group offers an early bid for risk appetite.

briefs.headline: Oil Firmer as Mideast Strikes Resume
briefs.body: Crude climbed following renewed US and Iran strikes across the region. The escalation kept geopolitical risk front of mind for traders overnight, with reports of casualties in Qatar from military operations. Energy markets remain sensitive to any further developments before the open.

briefs.headline: Gold Eases as Rate-Hike Bets Build
briefs.body: Gold slipped as firmer oil and growing expectations for Federal Reserve tightening weighed on the metal. The move underscores a market increasingly pricing higher-for-longer policy, complicating the usual safe-haven response to geopolitical stress.

briefs.headline: Healthcare Provides a Steady Hand
briefs.body: Healthcare ranked second among sectors, advancing close to 2 percent. A large pharmaceutical maker rose about 7 percent and a biotech climbed nearly 7 percent, lending the defensive group meaningful support as cyclicals wobbled.

briefs.headline: Asia Drifts on Mixed Cues
briefs.body: Regional markets traded without clear direction overnight as AI doubts and Middle East worries collided with rate-hike concerns. Indian shares held flat as US-Iran talks tempered escalation fears, leaving global investors waiting for a cleaner signal.
